title:
Association analysis of photoperiodic flowering time genes in west and central African sorghum [sorghum bicolor (L.)
Moench]
abstract:
Photoperiod-sensitive flowering is a key adaptive trait for sorghum (Sorghum bicolor) in
West and Central Africa. In this study we performed an association analysis to investigate the
effect of polymorphisms within the genes putatively related to variation in flowering time on
photoperiod-sensitive flowering in sorghum. For this purpose a genetically characterized
panel of 219 sorghum accessions from West and Central Africa was evaluated for their
photoperiod response index (PRI) based on two sowing dates under field conditions.
